speaker: interviewer
text: This is tape number four of the interview with Marianna Glazek. Can you tell us who this is?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, this is my-- my grandfather, Jacob Seeman, was his name. That's my father's father. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] These are-- this is the member of my family when my mother and-- and her sister were a young girl. This is in a vacation place in Hungary in-- in a hotel. You can see the sign of the hotel there. And my grandfather and my grandmother is on it and my great-grandmother and my great-grandfather and my-- my mother and her sister and her brother. This is also before I was born. It's my grandmother and my great-grandmother and their sister, I think. And this is either my aunt or my mother. I'm not sure. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] This is my-- my grandmother and my grandfather and then the two daughters, my-- my mother and her sister and their brother. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] This is my-- this is my sister and myself. It must be one of the summer vacation places. And my sister has a rose in front of her. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] This is myself. I'm in Switzerland in St. Moritz, and this was just when the war actually became pretty vicious in 1938. And I'm posing with my skis. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] This is my father when he was a young man. That was before he got married. I think this is dedicated to his sister-in-law.

speaker: interviewer
text: Full picture.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the last Passover that we spent together in Budapest. My older cousin was already in Switzerland, and we never had another Passover again together. We used to meet every year.

speaker: interviewer
text: Was this 1944?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, I think it's '43. This is a picture of my first husband, the one who disappeared in the-- after the-- the Holocaust, after the-- our stay in the Nazi house. [PAUSES FOR 8 SECONDS] This is my-- my husband, [? Yuri, ?] and his friend, Pauly. It's a ice skating rink. This was an artificial ice skating rink, and we went there all winter every other day. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] This was in the displaced person camp. And that man in the uniform is my husband and the little girl in my arms is Judy, my oldest daughter. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] This is myself and my husband. Just-- it was already during the war when they took that picture, 1943 maybe, before we got-- when we got married. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] This is my aunt and my uncle with their two sons in Venice on a vacation.

speaker: interviewer
text: What are their names?

speaker: interviewer
text: What are their names?

speaker: interviewee
text: Their name is [? Pishtar, ?] or Stephen, is the older one. And [? Leonchi, ?] or John, is the younger one. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] Amazing. This is my husband and myself after we got married in Austria.

speaker: interviewer
text: What was his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Edward M. Glazek is his name. This is my sister in her favorite pose. She was a gymnast. She was actually training for the Olympics, but then she didn't go because she was Jewish. And this is on a-- a vacation place on the lake. My mother looks like she's 16. This is my-- this is my mother and her sister, and it must have been early in the century. My mother looks like she's 16 maybe, both of them in their school girl's uniform. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] This is my sister and myself dressed exactly alike. I was 10 years old and she was eight years old at that time. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] This is my retirement. I received the professor emeritus status that I was told that I should be very proud of. [LAUGHS] [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: Dillon.

speaker: interviewer
text: Marianne, would you like to introduce us?

speaker: interviewee
text: Introduce my family. Yeah, well, maybe I'll start with Brian.

speaker: interviewee
text: Me.

speaker: interviewee
text: With my son-in-law. [LAUGHTER] And next to Brian-- next to Brian is who?

speaker: interviewee
text: The-- the-- the terrible boy.

speaker: interviewee
text: The terrible boy.

speaker: interviewee
text: Benjamin.

speaker: interviewee
text: Benjamin and Julia and my daughter, Judy, and my granddaughter, Alana. Dillon, Alex, and Carmen. This just part of my family.

speaker: interviewer
text: Would anybody like to say anything?

speaker: interviewee
text: I would like to say that I'm very proud of my Oma, that all that's she's gone through. And I'd just like to say I love her.

speaker: interviewer
text: I agree. [LAUGHTER]

speaker: interviewee
text: And I'm-- I'm the baby in the-- that was in the story, and if it wasn't for her wanting to survive, I wouldn't be here now. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Well, I want to thank you. Today has really been a privilege for me. It truly is Hitler's revenge, as you said. [LAUGHTER]

speaker: interviewee
text: It certainly is. It really was very nice that you came and doing this. It's wonderful. Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: This is the--

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you, both of you. [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: This is the conclusion of the videotape session. We used four tapes.
